resources.string: Another app is using the mic :(
"Learn to play the guitar, for free"
So that we can listen to you playing and give you feedback
We need access to the mic to do this exercise
The app does not detect what I play
Download a tuner app and tune it first!
Start playing in order to get a Score!
You got 3 Chordies, you\re better than before!
You got 4 Chordies, one step from success!

First of all, we are going to learn the basics about a guitar. What parts is it made of? How to grab it? How to push the strings? Make sure you have your guitar, and that you are in a comfortable place
Get comfy and grab the guitar like this
The one on top has the lowest pitch
And the one on the bottom has the highest pitch
Use your right hand to make the strings sound
On your left part there is the fretboard
Each cell is called a fret, and are used to change the sound of the strings
Use your left hand to push the strings on each fret
You should push right next to the metallic bar, but not on top of it!
That\'s all for now. Let\'s start!
Chords are used to make rhythms with the guitar. Virtually every song use them one way or another. Here you will learn to play A Major, one of the basics
Hi! I\'m Chordy. Here you will learn to play your fist chord: A Major!
Chords are used to produce rhythms, and they have different modalities that you will learn soon, for example: Minor, Seventh...
I will listen while you play and give you feedback. Grab the guitar, and press Play
This is the fretboard as you can see it on your left (just the first top frets). You can see A Major there
Put your fingers like that to make A Major. Do you see the red cross on the sixth string? That means you should not play that one
Make sure that all the strings sound nice. It will take a while for your fingers to adapt to it, but I promise it will feel super natural very soon
In the bottom each pick is a stroke, in this case a down stroke
You need to play the stroke when the pick reaches the line. I am listening, give it a try!
Great! Finish the exercise by playing the rest of strokes
The next one is D Major, chord that goes really well with A Major. These are two widely used chords and popular songs. Practice both, and soon you will be composing beautiful rhythms

The last chord we need before playing a proper song, is E Major. It is the most fully sounding, because it uses the six strings. You can play along A Major and D Major and they will always sound great

Now we go back to A Major, but you have to pay attention to the direction of the stroke. Play the strokes from up to down, and from down to up alternatively. Make sure you play it on time or you will lose points

It is the time for the final test: changing chords. Practice slowly how to change from one chord to the other. Also, play close attention to the direction of the stroke. Practice this a few times, and you will be a pro in no time
Let\'s go for a melody that I am sure you know already: the Ode to Joy. It is a must-know for everyone that wants to play the guitar
Now that we know the basics about chords, let\'s learn about melodies. Melodies are notes played one at a time (like a guitar solo)
Hi again! Now we are going to learn how to play melodies
The notes will appear in the fretboard as they need to be played
To start the exercise, press Play
Look out for the first note in the first string
The note is not on any fret, so you do not have to push any. That is called play an open string
When both circles touch, it is time to play the note. Try!
Good! Now you see the next note is in the first fret. Push it with your index finger and play the string
Awesome! This time, you have to play the second open string
Great! Last one, play the first fret on the second string. Happy playing!
